rationale: Keep: the citing paper’s core mechanism is that “subjective processing fluency partially mediates” knowledge-fit effects (Studies 2 and 4), and it explicitly situates itself within fluency accounts. The cited paper is a direct fluency mechanism source (introducing preference fluency and showing attribution-dependent fluency effects), so this looks plausibly foundational/theoretical rather than an incidental mention; the pair is also a direct DOI match.
